title: 7 MassDOT employees on leave after TV news investigation into overtime
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Seven MassDOT employees are on administrative leave after a Boston News 25 investigation into possible timesheet falsifications for overtime.
The investigation revealed high-earning employees left work early, yet claimed hours for tasks like yard organizing and truck cleaning.
MassDOT stresses accountability and legal compliance in time reporting; employee departures before shift end were documented by the investigation.

title: Theo Epstein shares his thoughts on state of 2026 Red Sox
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Theo Epstein believes the Red Sox can overcome their current 22-27 record with improvements in offense.
Epstein praised the team's pitching and defense, calling them strong foundations for the season.
Roman Anthony's recovery from a finger injury is delayed, affecting an already struggling lineup.

title: Person who attacked Harvard student from behind snuck into MIT dorm shortly after, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A Harvard student was attacked from behind in Lowell House by a suspect who later attempted entry into MIT's Sidney-Pacific dorm.
The suspect is described as a white male, 5-feet-9-inches tall, thin build, wearing a white t-shirt and dark shorts.
MIT Police are seeking information about the incident and have released an image of the suspect for public assistance.

title: Police: Woman, 85, found dead in R.I. hotel after son dies by suicide
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: 85-year-old Terry Costello found dead at Harbor Base Inn, Newport, after son's suicide.
Terry Costello's death investigated as homicide; autopsy by state's medical examiner pending.
Police treat incident as isolated; no active threat to public, according to Newport authorities.

title: If Bruins are looking to make a deal, the Ducks are an appealing trade partner
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are considering trading for Anaheim Ducks players Olen Zellweger and Mason McTavish for roster upgrades.
Zellweger, known for his speed, is a potential fit for the Bruins' defensive needs, providing offensive upside and mobility.
McTavish, a center with potential, could bolster the Bruins' depth despite recent struggles and a significant contract.

title: Shark season is here. One just popped up in a Rhode Island pond.
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A porbeagle shark was found stranded in shallow water at Point Judith Pond, Rhode Island, and later died.
The Atlantic Shark Institute observed multiple shark sightings in Narragansett, including the porbeagle at Salty Brine State Beach.
NOAA conducted a necropsy on the deceased shark and confirmed it presented no threat to people.

title: Families continue to fight BPS exam school admissions policy after judge rules no discrimination against white, Asian students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Parent Coalition appeals decision dismissing claims of racial discrimination in exam school admissions against white and Asian students.
New appeal argues Boston's socioeconomic-based admissions tiers act as racial proxies, impacting white and Asian student representation.
Federal judge previously upheld admissions changes, stating they don't violate equal protection; Supreme Court declined to review case.

title: Patriots receivers well aware of the A.J. Brown trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: A.J. Brown is rumored to join the New England Patriots from the Philadelphia Eagles after June 1.
Patriots' Kyle Williams and Kevin Byard commented on the trade rumors and praised Brown's skillset.
Eagles face a $43.3M cap hit if they trade Brown before June 1, reduced to $16.3M after.

title: Students in Maine were accidentally served dirt for dessert, school says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Three students at Medomak Valley High School unknowingly ate dirt instead of dessert during a school event on May 13.
The mix-up occurred when sterilized soil from a science project was mistaken for a food item at the Empty Bowl Supper.
School officials clarified the incident was accidental and contacted the parents of the affected students immediately.

title: What the Patriots are saying about Super Bowl rematch with Seahawks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots will face the Seahawks in a 2026 season opener, a rematch of their Super Bowl LX loss.
Super Bowl MVP Kenneth Walker III has left the Seahawks for Kansas City, impacting the upcoming rematch dynamics.
Patriots players express motivation and readiness, aiming for a different outcome following last year's Super Bowl defeat.
